skill.name: google-official-seo-guide
skill.description: Official Google SEO guide covering search optimization, best practices, Search Console, crawling, indexing, and improving website search visibility based on official Google documentation
skill.summary: Comprehensive SEO guide with Google Search optimization, structured data, technical SEO, and search visibility best practices
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: This skill consists entirely of markdown documentation files containing SEO guidance from Google's official documentation. All static scanner findings are false positives: (1) 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts are triggered by JSON-LD schema.org examples in documentation; (2) 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' alerts are triggered by markdown code block formatting; (3) 'Hardcoded URL' alerts are legitimate Google documentation URLs. No executable code or security risks present.

content.value_statement: Users struggle to optimize their websites for Google Search without authoritative guidance. This skill provides comprehensive SEO assistance based on official Google documentation, covering crawling, indexing, structured data, and technical SEO best practices.
actual_capabilities: Provide SEO recommendations based on official Google documentation
Help implement structured data (JSON-LD) for rich results
Explain crawling, indexing, and ranking processes
Guide on technical SEO including mobile-first indexing
Assist with Google Search Console usage and debugging
Recommend best practices for meta tags and content optimization
limitations: Does not make actual changes to user websites
Cannot access real-time Google Search data or rankings
No guaranteed ranking improvements - only follows Google guidelines
Does not replace professional SEO audit services for complex issues

use_cases.title: Implement VideoObject Structured Data
use_cases.description: Add proper JSON-LD schema markup to help videos appear as rich results in Google Search

use_cases.title: Debug Indexing Issues
use_cases.description: Identify why pages are not being indexed and provide solutions based on Google guidelines

use_cases.title: Optimize for Mobile-First Indexing
use_cases.description: Ensure website meets Google mobile-first requirements for better search visibility

prompt_templates.title: Basic SEO Question
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I improve my website's ranking in Google Search?

prompt_templates.title: Structured Data Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add VideoObject structured data to my video page. The video is at https://example.com/video.mp4
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to implement schema.org markup

prompt_templates.title: Indexing Debugging
prompt_templates.prompt: My pages are not appearing in Google Search. How do I troubleshoot indexing issues?

prompt_templates.title: Technical SEO Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: What technical SEO factors should I check for mobile-first indexing compliance?

output_examples.input: How do I create a sitemap for my website?
output_examples.output: A sitemap helps Google discover and index your pages. Create an XML sitemap following the sitemaps.org protocol. Include: page URLs, last modified dates, change frequency, and priority. Submit via Google Search Console. Avoid: listing non-canonical URLs, including blocked pages, or creating huge sitemaps over 50MB.

output_examples.input: Add structured data for a blog post
output_examples.output:
Here is JSON-LD structured data for a blog post:
```json
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "BlogPosting",
  "headline": "Your Post Title",
  "image": ["https://example.com/image.jpg"],
  "datePublished": "2024-01-15T08:00:00+08:00",
  "author": {
    "@type": "Person",
    "name": "Author Name"
  }
}
```
Place this in the <head> section of your HTML. Test with Google's Rich Results Test.

best_practices: Always verify recommendations with Google's official documentation
Use Google Search Console to monitor indexing and search performance
Focus on user-first content rather than keyword stuffing
anti_patterns: Do not guarantee specific ranking positions - Google algorithms change
Avoid participating in link schemes or buying links
Do not create duplicate content across multiple URLs without canonical tags

faq.question: How long does it take for Google to index my pages?
faq.answer: Indexing time varies from hours to weeks. Submit a sitemap in Google Search Console and ensure your pages are accessible via links.

faq.question: What is the most important ranking factor?
faq.answer: Google uses many factors. Focus on: relevant content, mobile-friendliness, page speed, secure HTTPS, and authoritative backlinks.

faq.question: Do I need to use structured data?
faq.answer: Structured data is not a ranking factor but helps Google understand your content and can enable rich results. It is recommended for certain content types.

faq.question: How do I check if my pages are indexed?
faq.answer: Use the site:yourdomain.com search operator or check Google Search Console Coverage report.

faq.question: What is mobile-first indexing?
faq.answer: Google primarily uses the mobile version of content for indexing and ranking. Ensure your mobile site has the same content as desktop.

faq.question: Should I use www or non-www URLs?
faq.answer: Choose one format and set up 301 redirects and canonical tags. Be consistent across your site.
